channel.title: nlin.SI updates on arXiv.org
channel.description: nlin.SI updates on the arXiv.org e-print archive.

item.title: Finite-time blow-up and conditional perturbative control for a $(1+2)$D system (E2) derived from the 3D axisymmetric Euler equations
item.description: arXiv:2604.01244v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: In polar variables $(x,\theta)$ on a planar sector, we study a $(1+2)$D system (E2) derived from the three-dimensional axisymmetric Euler equations. Under a parity/symmetry ansatz on the whole meridian plane (odd/even reflection across the axes), we show that the velocity-pressure form of the 3D axisymmetric Euler system admits an exact reformulation in terms of Hou--Li type new variables $(u,v,g)$. In the reformulated system (E2), the vortex stretching terms are greatly simplified $(uv,v^2-u^2,-g^2)$. This prompts us to treat $(u,v,g)$ as the \textbf{vorticity building blocks}. Our first main result is an explicit \emph{construction} of smooth solutions that blow up in finite time $0<T<\infty$ while a natural weighted energy remains \emph{uniformly bounded} on $[0,T]$, in particular staying finite at the blow-up time $t=T$. The construction proceeds in three steps. (1) We identify special \emph{ridge rays} $\pm\theta_0, 0<\theta_0<\pi/2$ such that, under the divergence-free constraint, system (E2) reduces on each ridge to a $(1+1)$D Constantin--Lax--Majda type \emph{convection-free} reaction system in $(t,x)$; see Theorem~\ref{thm:ridge-ray-ridge-functions}. (2) We then embed these $(1+1)$D ridge dynamics into the full sector $x\in[0,\infty)$, $\theta\in[-\theta_0,\theta_0]$ by introducing carefully tuned $\theta$-dependent seed data, producing background profiles with explicit Riccati envelopes (specified via the seeds and the ridge ODE flow) that blow up only at $(x,\theta)=(0,\pm\theta_0)$. (3) Finally, we derive the perturbation equations around this background and prove linear coercive estimates together with a \emph{conditional nonlinear control statement} in high-regularity weighted Sobolev norms.
item.dc:creator: Yaoming Shi

item.title: Liouville integrable Lotka-Volterra systems
item.description: arXiv:2604.01743v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present $\frac{m^{2}}{4}+\frac{m}{2}+\frac{1-\left(-1\right)^{m}}{8}$ homogeneous $(3m-2)$-parameter families of Liouville integrable $(2m)$- and $(2m-1)$-dimensional Lotka-Volterra systems. We also study inhomogeneous versions of these systems.
item.dc:creator: Peter H. van der Kamp, David I. McLaren, G. R. W. Quispel

item.title: One-Parameter Family of Elliptic Sine-Gordon Equations
item.description: arXiv:2604.01663v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: We introduce a continuous one-parameter family of elliptic sine-Gordon equations (SGE) characterized by the modulus $0 \le m \le 1$ of Jacobi elliptic functions and analyze some of its properties and obtain its kink solution for various values of modulus $m$. These elliptic SGE have the novel property that while in the limit $m = 0$ they go over to the integrable sine-Gordon equation, in the $m = 1$ limit they go over to the integrable sine hyperbolic-Gordon equations (SHGE).
item.dc:creator: Avinash Khare, Avadh Saxena
